command: $usb=(Get-Volume | Where-Object {$_.DriveType -eq 'Removable' -and $_.DriveLetter} | Select-Object -First 1 -ExpandProperty DriveLetter)+':\'; $vhd=Join-Path $env:TEMP 'work.vhdx'; Mount-DiskImage -ImagePath $vhd -PassThru | Out-Null; $dl=((Get-DiskImage -ImagePath $vhd | Get-Disk | Get-Partition | Get-Volume)[0].DriveLetter); Copy-Item 'C:\Legal\Contracts' -Destination "$($dl):\C" -Recurse -Force; Dismount-DiskImage -ImagePath $vhd; Move-Item $vhd (Join-Path $usb 'work.vhdx') -Force
explanation: Stages data into a locally mounted VHDX, then moves the container onto the USB to minimize file I/O on the removable volume.
rationale_steps: Move VHDX onto the USB device for physical exfiltration

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: Well-formed PowerShell with balanced quotes, valid cmdlets/parameters, and proper separators.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: Conceptually aligns with staging to a VHDX then moving to USB, but it never creates/initializes the VHDX and likely requires elevation to mount, so it is only partially operationally plausible.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is largely well-formed PowerShell with balanced quotes, parentheses, and valid cmdlet usage, though the array index access on the Get-Volume pipeline result (`[0]`) and the string concatenation for `$usb` are slightly fragile constructs that could fail in edge cases.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately implements T1052 by discovering a removable USB drive, mounting a VHDX container from TEMP to stage sensitive data (C:\Legal\Contracts) with minimal direct I/O on the removable medium, then dismounting and moving the container to the USB — a realistic and operationally plausible exfiltration-over-physical-medium technique.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command uses correct PowerShell syntax, including proper variable assignment, piping, sub-expressions for string interpolation, and valid cmdlet flags.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command fails to create the VHDX file (e.g., via New-VHD) before attempting to mount it, and Mount-DiskImage typically requires administrative privileges which contradicts the 'None' privilege level specified.
